findings.message: New Prometheus scrape job `redis` targets `redis-exporter:9121`, but no `redis-exporter` service exists in any Compose file (infra/compose/observability.yml, services.yml, etc.). The scrape target is unroutable and will produce a permanently-down target. Compare to `postgres`, whose `postgres-exporter` is defined in infra/compose/observability.yml.
findings.suggestion: Add a `redis-exporter` service (e.g. oliver006/redis_exporter) to infra/compose/observability.yml wired to the Redis instance, or remove the `redis` scrape job until the exporter exists.

findings.message: New `redis` scrape job has no matching alert rule. Every other scrape surface has a rule file in infra/observability/prometheus/alerts/ (postgres_alerts.yml, otel_collector_alerts.yml, prometheus_alerts.yml), but there is no redis_alerts.yml. Redis being scraped without any alert means failures (target down, high memory, evictions) go unnoticed.
findings.suggestion: Add infra/observability/prometheus/alerts/redis_alerts.yml with at least an up/availability alert (and ideally memory/eviction rules) for the redis job.

findings.message: New `redis` scrape job has no corresponding Grafana dashboard. Every other surface has a dashboard in infra/observability/grafana/dashboards/ (postgres.json, otel-collector.json, prometheus.json, slo.json), but there is no redis.json. Redis metrics are collected but not visualizable.
findings.suggestion: Add infra/observability/grafana/dashboards/redis.json with panels for the redis_exporter metrics, mirroring the postgres dashboard.
